findings.evidence: `RegularTorsor.lean:52-58` uses `hreg` only for `hreg.nonempty_fiber b`, `deckEquivFiber hp hreg e₂`, and `deckEquivFiber_symm_smul`; `Connected.lean:128-132` already has `deckEquivFiberOfSurjective hp e hsurj`, and `Regular.lean:150-153` shows `deckEquivFiber` is just this local equivalence with `MulAction.surjective_smul` from the fibre transitivity instance.
findings.fix: Prove a local version using `[Nonempty (p ⁻¹' {b})]` and `[MulAction.IsPretransitive (Deck p) (p ⁻¹' {b})]` (or an explicit orbit-map surjectivity hypothesis), using `deckEquivFiberOfSurjective`; then define the regular-cover `fiberTorsor` as a specialization via `hreg.fiber_isPretransitive b`.
findings.issue: `fiberTorsor` requires `hreg : IsRegular p`, which includes surjectivity of `p` and transitivity on every fibre, although this declaration packages only the fibre over `b`.

summary: The torsor package is stated only for globally regular maps, but the construction is fibre-local and the existing API already exposes the weaker local hypothesis needed. Generalize the main declaration first and derive the regular-cover specialization from it.
